Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Retrieve loan LN-2026-00619 using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number "LN-2026-00619") or mortgage_los_search_loans with a query containing that loan number, then pull borrower details using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id with the loan’s borrower_id (or mortgage_los_get_borrower with the borrower email) to confirm status (e.g. clear_to_close), closing_date, rate_lock_expiration, assigned_lo, assigned_processor, property address (9866 Albemarle Rd), and document/condition context via mortgage_los_get_conditions or mortgage_los_get_documents as needed. Fee amounts themselves are reconciled in Stripe and QuickBooks in later OEs.
OE 2: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "LN-2026-00619" or "Marcus Rivera" or "Albemarle" (or similar), optionally filter_in_channel for C005 (#rate-watch) or C002 (#loan-processing), to surface the lock-expiration and processing-delay thread, the pending $375 lock-extension placeholder discussion, and the fee-or-waiver confusion (including the task-changelog message on rate-watch when merged with base data).
OE 3: Search email using search_emails with query related to "LN-2026-00619" or "Marcus Rivera" or "Plaza" or "lock extension" (or similar), across INBOX and SENT as needed, to find borrower/lender correspondence and the Plaza Home Mortgage lock-extension fee and waiver thread.
OE 4: Find the QuickBooks bill for the $375 lock extension using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria matching DocNumber "PLAZA-LN2026-00619" or description containing LN-2026-00619 (or similar), then retrieve the bill with quickbooks_get_bill using the returned bill id (e.g. 5507224349 when supplied by the merged universe/changelog) to confirm Balance 375, DueDate 2026-04-17, and open/unpaid status versus any waiver narrative in email.
OE 5: Resolve Marcus Rivera’s CRM contact using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Marcus Rivera" (or crm_get_contact if the contact id is already known), then list engagements using crm_list_engagements with contact_ids containing that contact’s id, to find the engagement body that states no additional fees were expected—contradicting the open QuickBooks bill from OE 4.
OE 6: Look up Stripe using stripe_search_customers or stripe_list_customers for Marcus Rivera / LN-2026-00619, then list or search charges using stripe_list_charges or stripe_search_charges with query/description tied to LN-2026-00619 or "Rate lock extension" and Rivera, to compare charged or failed payment attempts against QuickBooks and to note the customer email (e.g. m.rivera@yahoo.com on the Stripe customer) versus the LOS borrower email (marcus.rivera.buyer@gmail.com).
OE 7: Look up Keystone email addresses using contacts_search_contacts with queries for Carlos Rivera, Sofia Reyes, Camille Foster, Hana Kim, Denise Holloway, and Marcus Rivera (borrower) before composing sends. For Slack posting, use channel_id C005 (#rate-watch) and C002 (#loan-processing) from slack channel data rather than expecting Slack user ids from Contacts.
OE 8: Send an email using send_email with sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and recipients including carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com, sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com, and camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com, covering root cause (stale or inactive LOS assignments versus live comms, expired lock, missed closing), current risks (borrower fee expectations vs open bill, compliance exposure), and a concrete owner-by-owner recovery plan with dates.
OE 9: Send an email using send_email with sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and recipient marcus.rivera.buyer@gmail.com (LOS borrower email), with plain-language explanation of missed closing, current loan status, next steps, and fee implications consistent with the reconciled Stripe, QuickBooks, and CRM picture.
OE 10: Confirm the LOS activity feed is empty or sparse using mortgage_los_get_activity_log with loan_id los_loan_850760aecd29 (resolved from mortgage_los_get_loan), then add documentation using mortgage_los_add_activity with that loan_id, required action and detail fields summarizing root cause, fee conflict, staff-assignment gap, and recovery plan.
OE 11: Add a new CRM engagement using crm_create_engagement on Marcus Rivera’s contact_ids with engagement_type and body/title describing the corrected situation, fee conflict, and recovery actions taken (superseding the prior “no additional fees” narrative without relying on a non-existent crm_update_engagement tool; optionally crm_delete_engagement on the stale engagement id only if policy and tools allow).
OE 12: Post a concise update using conversations_add_message with channel_id C005 (#rate-watch) and payload summarizing lock expiration status, the $375 fee conflict, and next steps.
OE 13: Post a concise update using conversations_add_message with channel_id C002 (#loan-processing) and payload summarizing missed closing, inactive or mismatched LOS staff assignment relative to live operations, outstanding document/condition items if any, and recovery ownership.
OE 14: Send an escalation email using send_email with sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and recipients hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com documenting the fee mismatch (open $375 QuickBooks bill versus CRM “no additional fees” language), waiver conflict, and compliance exposure from inactive LOS assignments on an active clear-to-close file.
OE 15: Send a final summary email using send_email with recipient grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (internal ops summary on behalf of the requester), containing the full timeline reconstruction, recommendation on closing or next gates for the file, and items needing executive attention.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Fee amounts themselves are reconciled in Stripe and QuickBooks in later OEs.
OE 2: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "LN-2026-00619" or "Marcus Rivera" or "Albemarle" (or similar), optionally filter_in_channel for C005 (#rate-watch) or C002 (#loan-processing), to surface the lock-expiration and processing-delay thread, the pending $375 lock-extension placeholder discussion, and the fee-or-waiver confusion (including the task-changelog message on rate-watch when merged with base data).

OE 4: Find the QuickBooks bill for the $375 lock extension using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria matching DocNumber "PLAZA-LN2026-00619" or description containing LN-2026-00619 (or similar), then retrieve the bill with quickbooks_get_bill using the returned bill id (e.g. 5507224349 when supplied by the merged universe/changelog) to confirm Balance 375, DueDate 2026-04-17, and open/unpaid status versus any waiver narrative in email.
OE 5: Resolve Marcus Rivera’s CRM contact using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Marcus Rivera" (or crm_get_contact if the contact id is already known), then list engagements using crm_list_engagements with contact_ids containing that contact’s id, to find the engagement body that states no additional fees were expected—contradicting the open QuickBooks bill from OE 4.
OE 6: Look up Stripe using stripe_search_customers or stripe_list_customers for Marcus Rivera / LN-2026-00619, then list or search charges using stripe_list_charges or stripe_search_charges with query/description tied to LN-2026-00619 or "Rate lock extension" and Rivera, to compare charged or failed payment attempts against QuickBooks and to note the customer email (e.g. m.rivera@yahoo.com on the Stripe customer) versus the LOS borrower email (marcus.rivera.buyer@gmail.com).
OE 7: Look up Keystone email addresses using contacts_search_contacts with queries for Carlos Rivera, Sofia Reyes, Camille Foster, Hana Kim, Denise Holloway, and Marcus Rivera (borrower) before composing sends. For Slack posting, use channel_id C005 (#rate-watch) and C002 (#loan-processing) from slack channel data rather than expecting Slack user ids from Contacts.
OE 8: Send an email using send_email with sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and recipients including carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com, sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com, and camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com, covering root cause (stale or inactive LOS assignments versus live comms, expired lock, missed closing), current risks (borrower fee expectations vs open bill, compliance exposure), and a concrete owner-by-owner recovery plan with dates.
OE 9: Send an email using send_email with sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and recipient marcus.rivera.buyer@gmail.com (LOS borrower email), with plain-language explanation of missed closing, current loan status, next steps, and fee implications consistent with the reconciled Stripe, QuickBooks, and CRM picture.
OE 10: Confirm the LOS activity feed is empty or sparse using mortgage_los_get_activity_log with loan_id los_loan_850760aecd29 (resolved from mortgage_los_get_loan), then add documentation using mortgage_los_add_activity with that loan_id, required action and detail fields summarizing root cause, fee conflict, staff-assignment gap, and recovery plan.
OE 11: Add a new CRM engagement using crm_create_engagement on Marcus Rivera’s contact_ids with engagement_type and body/title describing the corrected situation, fee conflict, and recovery actions taken (superseding the prior “no additional fees” narrative without relying on a non-existent crm_update_engagement tool; optionally crm_delete_engagement on the stale engagement id only if policy and tools allow).
OE 12: Post a concise update using conversations_add_message with channel_id C005 (#rate-watch) and payload summarizing lock expiration status, the $375 fee conflict, and next steps.
OE 13: Post a concise update using conversations_add_message with channel_id C002 (#loan-processing) and payload summarizing missed closing, inactive or mismatched LOS staff assignment relative to live operations, outstanding document/condition items if any, and recovery ownership.
OE 14: Send an escalation email using send_email with sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and recipients hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com documenting the fee mismatch (open $375 QuickBooks bill versus CRM “no additional fees” language), waiver conflict, and compliance exposure from inactive LOS assignments on an active clear-to-close file.
OE 15: Send a final summary email using send_email with recipient grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (internal ops summary on behalf of the requester), containing the full timeline reconstruction, recommendation on closing or next gates for the file, and items needing executive attention.

1 An email was sent to carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com, sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com, and camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com (together or separately).

outcome

4 The LN-2026-00619 loan record update documents at least one of: the root cause of the missed closing, the inactive staff assignment, the $375 fee conflict, or the recovery actions taken.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The activity log entries explicitly mention Brian Mitchell, Andre Washington (terminated), the $375 fee conflict, the missed closing on 3/17, and the recovery plan. Both the compliance_alert and note detail entries are substantive.
✓Run #2The loan record update (mortgage_los_update_loan notes field) explicitly documents root cause (Mitchell/Washington terminated), the missed 3/17 closing, $375 fee conflict, and recovery actions taken.
✓Run #3The compliance_alert activity log entry explicitly names Brian Mitchell (terminated), Andre Washington (terminated), the $825 vs $375 fee discrepancy, and the recovery actions taken.
✓Run #4The successful note activity log entry explicitly mentions Brian Mitchell (terminated 4/15/2025), Andre Washington (terminated 12/15/2024), the $375 fee mismatch, and the missed closing date of 3/17.
✓Run #5The mortgage_los_update_loan notes field contains extensive detail including Brian Mitchell terminated 4/15/2025, Andre Washington terminated 12/15/2024, $825 Stripe vs $375 QB fee discrepancy, missed Mar 17 closing, and recovery actions taken.
✓Run #6The mortgage_los_update_loan notes explicitly mention Mitchell (terminated), Washington (terminated), the $375 fee mismatch, and the recovery plan. The activity log entries also reference these details.
5 A new CRM engagement was created using crm_create_engagement for Marcus Rivera's CRM contact (contact_ids including d2be6573ce2d48e7b844983cf6b0445f from task changelog, or full_name/email match for Marcus Rivera) documenting the current situation (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2crm_create_engagement was called with contact_ids: ['d2be6573ce2d48e7b844983cf6b0445f'] (Marcus Rivera's CRM ID), documenting the recovery audit, fee mismatch, and compliance findings.
✓Run #3crm_create_engagement called with contact_ids ['d2be6573ce2d48e7b844983cf6b0445f'] (Marcus Rivera's CRM ID confirmed from crm_search_contacts result).
✓Run #4crm_create_engagement was called with contact_ids ['d2be6573ce2d48e7b844983cf6b0445f'] (Marcus Rivera's CRM contact ID) and succeeded with engagement id fb749c8db6224c488a48d8ed143a1b26.
✓Run #5crm_create_engagement was called with contact_ids ['d2be6573ce2d48e7b844983cf6b0445f'] (Marcus Rivera's contact ID), creating a NOTE engagement titled 'Recovery Audit - LN-2026-00619 (Marcus Rivera)'.
✓Run #6crm_create_engagement was called with contact_ids ['d2be6573ce2d48e7b844983cf6b0445f'] (Marcus Rivera's CRM contact ID) and succeeded, creating engagement id ba2ec3a16c0143489f3c2d5f67e89093.
6 The CRM engagement does not simply restate "no additional fees expected" without addressing the open $375 QuickBooks bill.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The CRM engagement body explicitly mentions 'borrower charged $375 for waived extension + $450 for $375 extension' and 'No QuickBooks records for lock fees.' It does not repeat 'no additional fees expected' and directly addresses the fee conflict.
✓Run #2The new CRM engagement body explicitly calls out the fee mismatch: 'Borrower charged $825 in lock extension fees...but was told no additional fees expected per CRM call log' and explicitly says 'if 1st extension was waived, the $375 Stripe charge must be refunded.' It does not repeat the old 'no additional fees expected' as current fact.
✓Run #3The CRM engagement body explicitly states 'borrower was told no additional fees but was charged $825 in lock extension fees via Stripe. Lender waived 1st extension. Audit in progress with Hana Kim.' It directly addresses the open $375 QB bill conflict.

✓Run #2quickbooks_search_bills was called with criteria filtering VendorRef.name LIKE '%Plaza%', which is a targeted search that identifies the Plaza Home Mortgage vendor. The criterion allows 'or similar' and this effectively targets the only Plaza-related bill in the system.
✓Run #3quickbooks_search_bills called twice with targeted criteria: first with VendorRef.name LIKE '%Plaza%' and second with DocNumber LIKE '%619%' — the second directly targets LN-2026-00619 by its doc number fragment and returned the PLAZA-LN2026-00619 bill.

✗Run #6quickbooks_search_bills was called with criteria [{"field": "VendorRef", "operator": "LIKE", "value": "%Plaza%"}]. The query uses vendor name, not 'LN-2026-00619', 'PLAZA-LN2026-00619', or 'Marcus Rivera' as required by the criterion.

24 If the send_email body to [marcus.rivera.buyer@gmail.com](mailto:marcus.rivera.buyer@gmail.com) reassures there are \\"no fees, "no additional fees," or equivalent, it must also mention the $375 QuickBooks bill (DocNumber PLAZA-LN2026-00619), a documented waiver, or uncertainty/pending resolution—not a blanket no-fee promise alone (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The borrower email says 'We are reviewing all charges associated with your file, including rate lock extension fees. If we find that any fees were charged incorrectly, we will make it right.' This avoids a blanket no-fee promise and acknowledges pending fee review/resolution rather than promising no fees.
✓Run #2The borrower email does NOT make a blanket no-fee promise. It says 'We are also conducting an internal review of the fees on your account. If we find that any charges were applied in error or inconsistent with what was communicated to you, we will correct that promptly and make it right.' This appropriately signals uncertainty and pending review rather than promising no fees.
✓Run #3Borrower email does not contain an unconditional no-fee promise. Instead it says 'We are also conducting a thorough review of all fees on your account to make sure everything is accurate and fair. You will receive a complete and transparent accounting within 48 hours.' No blanket no-fee language present.
✓Run #4The borrower email does not make a blanket 'no additional fees' promise. Instead it says: 'We are currently reviewing all fees associated with the rate lock extensions on your file. If we determine that any fee was charged in error or is owed back to you, we will address that promptly and transparently.' This appropriately conveys uncertainty rather than a no-fee promise.
✓Run #5The borrower email does not make a blanket no-fee promise. Instead it says 'I am personally reviewing this with our accounting and compliance teams... I will follow up with you directly once that review is complete — you do not need to take any action on fees right now.' This properly flags the fee situation as under review rather than making unconditional promises.
✓Run #6The borrower email does not make a blanket no-fee promise. It says 'I am personally reviewing all charges on your file. If any fees were applied that should not have been, we will correct that.' This acknowledges fee uncertainty rather than unconditionally promising no fees.
